description: Encountered a git push failure and spent extensive time troubleshooting with verbose flags, SSH configuration diagnostics, host key verification, DNS investigation, and multiple authentication approaches. After significant effort, user executed 'git push origin main' directly with no issues, immediately succeeding. User feedback: 'There is no connection issue' and 'I just pushed manually with no issue.' Critical failure: made a simple operation unnecessarily complex through excessive diagnostic troubleshooting instead of attempting basic operation first.
correction: Always apply Occam's Razor to operational failures. When a straightforward command fails, attempt the basic version first (git push origin main) before engaging in diagnostic troubleshooting. Do not over-complicate simple operations with verbose flags and system diagnostics.
systemic_fix: Rule: For operational tasks, execute the simplest possible version first. Only escalate to diagnostic troubleshooting if the basic operation demonstrably fails. Do not assume infrastructure problems — verify by attempting the basic operation. This prevents wasted context and user frustration on unnecessary complexity.
